edges.description: The gain-of-function potassium or loss-of-function calcium variant shifts the net current balance toward repolarization, abbreviating the action potential and refractory period in working myocardium.
Shortened refractory periods and impaired adaptation of repolarization favor rotor stabilization and re-entry in atrial and ventricular tissue.
The re-entry-prone substrate manifests clinically as atrial fibrillation, ventricular fibrillation, syncope, and sudden cardiac death.
Sustained ventricular fibrillation abolishes effective cardiac output, producing syncope and, if not terminated, sudden cardiac death.

nodes.description: The core electrophysiologic lesion in SQTS is accelerated repolarization, typically due to increased repolarizing potassium current in ventricular myocardium. This abbreviates action potential duration and refractory periods, producing the characteristic shortened QT interval and reducing the margin against re-entry.

biological_processes: membrane repolarization during cardiac muscle cell action potential
ventricular cardiac muscle cell action potential

evidence.reference_title: Sudden death associated with short-QT syndrome linked to mutations in HERG.
evidence.snippet: The mutations dramatically increase IKr, leading to heterogeneous abbreviation of action potential duration and refractoriness, and reduce the affinity of the channels to IKr blockers.

evidence.reference_title: Modeling Short QT Syndrome Using Human-Induced Pluripotent Stem Cell-Derived Cardiomyocytes.
evidence.snippet: The hiPSC-CMs from the patient showed increased rapidly activating delayed rectifier potassium channel current (IKr) density and shortened action potential duration compared with healthy control hiPSC-CMs.

evidence.reference_title: Modulation of I(Kr) inactivation by mutation N588K in KCNH2: a link to arrhythmogenesis in short QT syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Physiologically the N588K mutation abolishes rectification of HERG currents and specifically increases I(Kr) in the ventricle with minimal effects on the Purkinje fiber action potential duration.

nodes.description: SQTS clinically presents with malignant atrial and ventricular arrhythmias. Atrial fibrillation is common and may be the presenting feature, whereas ventricular fibrillation underlies aborted cardiac arrest and sudden cardiac death.

evidence.reference_title: Long-Term Follow-Up of Patients With Short QT Syndrome: Clinical Profile and Outcome.
evidence.snippet: Five patients had syncope (29%) and 9 (53%) had atrial fibrillation or atrial flutter.

evidence.reference_title: Short QT Syndrome: a familial cause of sudden death.
evidence.snippet: The short QT syndrome is characterized by familial sudden death, short refractory periods, and inducible ventricular fibrillation.

evidence.reference_title: Long-Term Follow-Up of Patients With Short QT Syndrome: Clinical Profile and Outcome.
evidence.snippet: An SQTS -related gene was found in 76% of the patients as follows: KCNH 2 ( SQTS 1) in 4, CACNA 1C ( SQTS 4) in 3, and CACN b2 ( SQTS 5) in 6.

evidence.reference_title: Long-Term Follow-Up of Patients With Short QT Syndrome: Clinical Profile and Outcome.
evidence.snippet: An SQTS -related gene was found in 76% of the patients as follows: KCNH 2 ( SQTS 1) in 4, CACNA 1C ( SQTS 4) in 3, and CACN b2 ( SQTS 5) in 6.

nodes.description: The upstream trigger of SQTS is a pathogenic germline variant that alters a cardiac ion channel. Gain-of-function variants in the potassium channels KCNH2 (IKr), KCNQ1 (IKs), and KCNJ2 (IK1) increase repolarizing current, while loss-of-function variants in the L-type calcium channel subunits CACNA1C and CACNB2 reduce inward calcium current. Either route shifts the net balance of currents toward accelerated repolarization in structurally normal myocardium.

evidence.reference_title: Sudden death associated with short-QT syndrome linked to mutations in HERG.
evidence.snippet: In 2 of them, we identified 2 different missense mutations resulting in the same amino acid change (N588K) in the S5-P loop region of the cardiac IKr channel HERG (KCNH2).

evidence.reference_title: Modulation of I(Kr) inactivation by mutation N588K in KCNH2: a link to arrhythmogenesis in short QT syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Physiologically the N588K mutation abolishes rectification of HERG currents and specifically increases I(Kr) in the ventricle with minimal effects on the Purkinje fiber action potential duration.

evidence.reference_title: Sudden death associated with short-QT syndrome linked to mutations in HERG.
evidence.snippet: In 2 of them, we identified 2 different missense mutations resulting in the same amino acid change (N588K) in the S5-P loop region of the cardiac IKr channel HERG (KCNH2).

evidence.reference_title: A novel form of short QT syndrome (SQT3) is caused by a mutation in the KCNJ2 gene.
evidence.snippet: The affected members of a single family had a G514A substitution in the KCNJ2 gene that resulted in a change from aspartic acid to asparagine at position 172 (D172N).
KCNJ2 | HGNC:6263 | short QT syndrome | MONDO:0000453 | AD | Moderate

evidence.reference_title: Mutation in the KCNQ1 gene leading to the short QT-interval syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Functional and computational studies of the KCNQ1 V307L mutation identified in a patient with this disorder favor the association of short QT with mutation in KCNQ1.
KCNQ1 | HGNC:6294 | short QT syndrome | MONDO:0000453 | AD | Strong

nodes.description: Abbreviated refractory periods and impaired rate adaptation create a myocardial substrate that is vulnerable to atrial and ventricular fibrillation. At the tissue level this is expressed as shortened excitation wavelength, enhanced inducibility of sustained spiral waves, and increased ventricular vulnerability to fibrillation.

evidence.reference_title: Short QT Syndrome: a familial cause of sudden death.
evidence.snippet: During electrophysiological study, short atrial and ventricular refractory periods were documented in all and increased ventricular vulnerability to fibrillation in 3 of 4 patients.

evidence.reference_title: Modeling Reentry in the Short QT Syndrome With Human-Induced Pluripotent Stem Cell-Derived Cardiac Cell Sheets.
evidence.snippet: Optical mapping of the SQTS-hiPSC-CCSs revealed shortened APD, impaired APD-rate adaptation, abbreviated wavelength of excitation, and increased inducibility of sustained spiral waves.

nodes.description: Loss of effective cardiac output from sustained ventricular fibrillation (or self-terminating tachyarrhythmia) causes transient cerebral hypoperfusion and syncope and, when the rhythm does not terminate, sudden cardiac death. These are the shared clinical endpoints of SQTS and are frequently the sentinel manifestation, occurring in infants, children, and young adults with structurally normal hearts.

evidence.reference_title: Long-Term Follow-Up of Patients With Short QT Syndrome: Clinical Profile and Outcome.
evidence.snippet: Short QT syndrome ( SQTS ) is a rare inheritable disease associated with sudden cardiac death.

evidence.reference_title: Short QT Syndrome: a familial cause of sudden death.
evidence.snippet: The short QT syndrome is characterized by familial sudden death, short refractory periods, and inducible ventricular fibrillation.
